mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to a negative outlook for equity valuations and driving down S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during periods of high inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon as economic expansion continues.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods where PAYEMS growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration or contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and overall economic slowdown, negatively impacting corporate revenues and leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during periods of industrial contraction.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects healthy consumer demand and confidence, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of periods where RSAFS growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate begins to rise significantly while the Federal Reserve is simultaneously tightening monetary policy, it signals a potential recessionary environment, leading to substantial negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate profits are expected to decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS has risen > 0.25% in 3 months
regime_hint: monetary tightening with labor market weakening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the combined signal's Rank IC is not significantly negative during periods of rising unemployment and tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts below a critical threshold indicates a slowdown in the construction sector and broader economic activity, which negatively impacts related industries and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during housing slowdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Robust real GDP growth signifies a healthy and expanding economy, which underpins strong corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a medium-term horizon of 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods where GDPC1 growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above a low threshold, it signals the beginning or continuation of a monetary tightening cycle, increasing the cost of capital and discounting future earnings more aggressively, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances during tightening cycles, or the Rank IC is not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS is above the threshold.
